message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_cov composes through mean_dim+matmul for autograd [bd-1tax]

tensor_cov was severed-autograd: extracted tensor_values, computed
the mean + (x-mean)(x-mean)^T sum in plain f64 loops, and rebuilt a
fresh requires_grad=false leaf — silently dropping gradients to the
input. PyTorch's torch.cov is differentiable. Same pattern fix as
recent xlogy / xlog1py / logit / entr rounds.

Composition (autograd-aware end-to-end):

    means    = mean_dim(input, dim=1)        // shape [N]
    means_b  = expand(unsqueeze(means, 1), [N, M])
    centered = input - means_b               // shape [N, M]
    cov      = (centered @ centered.T) / (M - 1)   // shape [N, N]

Implemented through tensor_mean_dim + tensor_unsqueeze +
tensor_expand + tensor_sub + tensor_matmul + tensor_transpose +
tensor_div, all of which already have backward kernels.

Test: cov_propagates_gradients_through_input asserts on a 2x3
input that backward produces finite gradients AND that each row's
gradient components sum to ~0 — cov is shift-invariant along the
observation dim, so adding a constant to row i must not change the
covariance, which under sum loss means d(sum(cov))/d(x[i,*]) sums
to zero per row. This catches both severed-tape regressions
(grad would be None) and any algebraic mistakes in the
composition (the row-sum invariant would break).

All 3 existing cov tests (cov_identity_correlation, cov_uncorrelated,
cov_1d_errors) continue to pass — bit-for-bit forward parity.

Files closure for one slice of frankentorch-1tax (umbrella
"statistical/special ops with severed-autograd" tracking bead).
Sister fix to corrcoef will follow naturally since corrcoef
delegates to cov internally.

Workspace: 1126 ft-api tests green (1125 + 1 new), clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
